name: Biloxi
profile: Military-friendly Keesler AFB housing snapshot anchored to Biloxi, Gulfport, Ocean Springs, D'Iberville, Long Beach, and the Mississippi Gulf Coast, built for PCSUnited base-level market overviews.

market_metrics.market_type_summary: Affordable Gulf Coast military and training market with coastal insurance sensitivity, flood-zone variation, and strong neighborhood-by-neighborhood differences

mortgage_assumptions.defaults_note: Based on March 2026 Mississippi Gulf Coast VA loan planning assumptions.

notes: Mississippi Gulf Coast ownership cost must include homeowners insurance, wind/hurricane exposure, flood-zone review, roof age, elevation, and storm-resilience considerations.
Keesler buyers should compare mortgage, taxes, insurance, flood insurance, HOA, utilities, commute, property condition, and storm-readiness together.
Biloxi, Gulfport, D'Iberville, Ocean Springs, Long Beach, and Woolmarket can each produce very different monthly, commute, and resale outcomes.

avg_home_mortgage_monthly.notes: VA loan planning estimate for practical Keesler / Biloxi-area housing, including Mississippi Gulf Coast insurance assumptions.

crime_status.installation: Extremely safe; gated military installation with controlled access.
crime_status.metro_biloxi_gulfport: Biloxi and the broader Mississippi Gulf Coast vary by neighborhood, flood zone, tourism exposure, and property condition. Military families should evaluate exact address, school zone, insurance, flood risk, commute, and resale logic before committing.

school_quality.on_base: No full on-base K-12 system should be assumed; families should verify current school options and attendance boundaries by exact housing address.
school_quality.off_base: Biloxi, Gulfport, Ocean Springs, D'Iberville, Long Beach, and Harrison/Jackson County school options vary by exact address. Families should verify district, school assignment, commute, and support services before choosing housing.

rental_vacancy.notes: Rental inventory is influenced by Keesler training turnover, casinos/tourism, shipbuilding, healthcare, military retirees, and broader Gulf Coast demand.

rental_metrics.notes: Planning estimate for 3-bedroom military-family rentals across Biloxi, Gulfport, D'Iberville, Long Beach, and nearby Gulf Coast corridors.

special_events: Keesler AFB community and training events
Gulf Coast Carnival / Mardi Gras events

military_lifestyle_fit.description: Major Air Education and Training Command hub with strong technical training identity, medical mission presence, Gulf Coast lifestyle, and affordable housing compared with many coastal military markets.

financial_brief.affordability_summary: Keesler is financially accessible by purchase price, but Gulf Coast insurance, flood-zone exposure, roof condition, and storm risk must be modeled before recommending buy.
financial_brief.buyer_opportunity: Strongest for disciplined VA buyers comparing Biloxi, D'Iberville, Gulfport, Long Beach, Woolmarket, and Ocean Springs by commute, insurance, schools, flood risk, and all-in payment.

market_bluf.bluf_summary: Keesler gives military families strong payment accessibility and coastal lifestyle value, but the best housing move requires discipline around insurance, flood zones, storm exposure, roof age, and property condition.

summary_points: Keesler AFB sits inside an affordable Gulf Coast market with strong military, training, medical, tourism, casino, shipbuilding, and retiree demand.
Biloxi offers the closest practical housing lane, while Gulfport, D'Iberville, Long Beach, Ocean Springs, and Woolmarket create different affordability and lifestyle tradeoffs.
Purchase prices can be attractive, but homeowners insurance, flood insurance, roof age, elevation, and storm readiness can materially change the monthly answer.
The best homes are not simply the cheapest homes; they are the ones with clean condition, insurable roof, sensible flood profile, and good resale logic.
The best Keesler housing decision balances BAH, commute, schools, insurance, flood risk, property condition, and assignment length.
opportunities: Lower purchase prices than many U.S. coastal military markets.
Short commute options in Biloxi and D'Iberville can improve daily quality of life.
Military training turnover supports rental demand for clean, practical homes.
Gulfport, Long Beach, and Woolmarket can offer more space and value depending on commute tolerance.
Ocean Springs may provide stronger lifestyle appeal for buyers with more payment room.
risks: Flood-zone exposure and wind/hurricane insurance can materially alter affordability.
Older roofs may create insurance or financing issues.
Tourism, casino, and coastal pockets can affect neighborhood feel and pricing.
School quality and neighborhood fit vary by exact address.
Short assignments may favor renting or on-base housing if resale and transaction-cost risk are high.
buyer_notes: Keesler buyers should compare Biloxi, D'Iberville, Gulfport, Long Beach, Woolmarket, and Ocean Springs by commute, school zones, insurance, flood risk, and all-in payment.
Do not judge affordability by purchase price alone; wind, flood, and homeowners insurance can shift the real answer.
Roof age, elevation, flood zone, HVAC, drainage, and storm-readiness should be reviewed before making an offer.
Ocean Springs can offer stronger lifestyle appeal but may cost more and add commute.
This market rewards buyers who keep the monthly numbers tight and avoid buying homes with hidden storm or insurance risk.
seller_notes: Sellers should position homes around commute logic, insurance clarity, roof age, flood-zone status, VA readiness, and school-zone appeal.
Move-in-ready homes with updated roof, HVAC, windows, elevation/flood clarity, and clean wind-mitigation-style documentation can stand out.
Military buyers respond well to practical monthly value and PCS-friendly timing.
Homes in strong commute corridors should emphasize Keesler access, insurability, storm-readiness, and low-maintenance ownership.
investor_angles: Military training turnover, tourism, casinos, healthcare, shipbuilding, and retiree demand support rental relevance across the Mississippi Gulf Coast.
Cash flow may be practical because acquisition prices are lower than many coastal markets.
Investors should stress-test insurance, flood zones, roof age, vacancy, tenant turnover, storm repairs, and property management.
Clean single-family homes in practical commute corridors may outperform cheap properties with roof, flood, or deferred-maintenance issues.
buyer_guidance: Use all-in monthly cost as the primary decision filter.
Compare Biloxi, D'Iberville, Gulfport, Long Beach, Woolmarket, Ocean Springs, and St. Martin by commute, school zone, flood risk, insurance, roof age, and resale appeal.
Verify homeowners insurance, flood insurance, flood zone, elevation, roof age, wind/hurricane exposure, drainage, and utility expectations before finalizing a purchase budget.
Avoid choosing only by lowest price if the home has roof, flood, insurance, or deferred-maintenance risk.
Use PCSUnited calculators to compare rent, buy, residual income, commute risk, insurance pressure, and assignment-length exposure.
seller_guidance: Price against current buyer payment sensitivity and insurance awareness.
Highlight commute logic, VA readiness, flood-zone clarity, roof age, storm-readiness, school-zone appeal, and low-maintenance condition.
Homes with updated roof, HVAC, windows, drainage, elevation clarity, and insurance-friendly documentation should be positioned clearly.
Military buyers need practical value and confidence in monthly cost, not just Gulf Coast lifestyle language.
landlord_notes: Military student and permanent-party demand can support occupancy near Keesler AFB.
Single-family rentals in good commute corridors may appeal to military families avoiding purchase risk.
Investors should model insurance, flood exposure, roof life, storm repairs, maintenance, vacancy, and turnover conservatively.
Clean, functional homes near base, services, and schools may outperform cheaper but weaker-condition inventory.

market.market_type_summary: Affordable Mississippi Gulf Coast military market with coastal insurance sensitivity and strong neighborhood variation

base_profile.market_label: Biloxi / Keesler Gulf Coast Military Housing Market
base_profile.installation_type: Technical Training / Medical / Cyber and Mission Support Base
base_profile.host_or_major_command: 81st Training Wing / Air Education and Training Command
base_profile.primary_mission_summary: Keesler AFB is a major Air Education and Training Command installation in Biloxi supporting technical training, cyber and communications training, medical missions, weather training, mission support, and a large student/permanent-party military population on the Mississippi Gulf Coast.
base_profile.pcs_personality: Coastal, training-heavy, affordable, flood/insurance-sensitive, family-relevant, student-turnover-aware, and strongly tied to Biloxi, Gulfport, D'Iberville, Ocean Springs, Long Beach, and the Mississippi Gulf Coast.
base_profile.base_bluf: Keesler is not just a low-cost coastal PCS. It is a BAH, training-turnover, insurance, flood-zone, storm-readiness, roof-condition, school-zone, and assignment-length decision. The payment can look great, but the house must survive Gulf Coast risk.

user_positioning.military_family_bluf: Prioritize flood-zone review, insurance quotes, roof age, school-zone verification, commute, property condition, BAH fit, and assignment length before signing a lease or purchase contract.
user_positioning.single_airman_bluf: Prioritize short commute, rent control, roommate flexibility, base/student convenience, and avoiding overbuying if the assignment or training timeline is short.
user_positioning.realtor_bluf: The strongest Keesler housing conversations are about insurance clarity, flood-zone status, roof condition, VA readiness, school-zone confidence, and realistic exit strategy.
user_positioning.investor_bluf: Rental demand near Keesler is influenced by student turnover, permanent party, healthcare, casinos/tourism, shipbuilding, retirees, and demand for clean single-family homes in practical commute corridors.

visitor_control_center.name: Keesler AFB Visitor Center
visitor_control_center.address: Division Street / White Avenue Gate visitor processing area, Keesler AFB, MS
visitor_control_center.hours: Monday-Friday, 6:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m.; closed weekends, AETC family days, and federal holidays; verify current hours before travel
best_for: Family members without routine installation access
visitor_control_center.map_zone: Division Street / White Avenue Gate visitor processing area
visitor_control_center.note: Use official Keesler AFB visitor information before arrival. Access requirements, sponsor rules, gate procedures, and hours can change.

gates.hours: Verify official Keesler AFB gate hours before travel

gates.commute_notes: Primary visitor and newcomer access logic for many users approaching from Biloxi and coastal corridors.
gates.user_warning: Verify current gate status with official Keesler AFB sources before travel.

gates.name: Pass Road Gate
gates.hours: Verify official Keesler AFB gate hours before travel
best_for: Users approaching from west/east coastal corridors
gates.commute_notes: May support daily commuting depending on final duty location and current gate access rules.
gates.user_warning: Gate hours and route suitability should be verified before relying on this gate.

gates.hours: Verify official Keesler AFB gate hours before travel

on_base_housing.housing_type: Privatized and military housing support environment
on_base_housing.housing_office_email: Verify current housing office email through official sources
on_base_housing.housing_office_hours: Monday-Thursday, 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m.; Friday, 8:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m. CST; verify current hours
on_base_housing.map_zone: housing support area / Keesler Family Housing
best_for: Families wanting to reduce commute uncertainty
Users wanting to reduce coastal insurance/flood-risk complexity
watchouts: Availability and waitlists can change
On-base housing may capture BAH differently than off-base renting
Pet policies and breed restrictions should be verified
School assignment must be verified by exact address
Off-base options in Biloxi, D'Iberville, Gulfport, Long Beach, and Ocean Springs should be compared carefully
Gulf Coast insurance, flood risk, and storm readiness should be modeled for off-base purchase scenarios
on_base_housing.pcsu_strategy_note: Compare on-base housing against off-base rent and mortgage options. Around Keesler, the best choice depends on rank, dependents, BAH, debt, commute tolerance, school priorities, insurance and flood risk, roof condition, training/permanent-party status, pets, and expected assignment length.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Biloxi Practical Pockets
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Closest practical Keesler-connected housing lane with short commute, coastal access, and strong base relevance.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 5-20 minutes depending on exact address, gate, traffic, and flood-prone routes
likely_gate_logic: Pass Road Gate depending on route and current access rules
watchouts: Flood and insurance should be checked carefully
Neighborhood and property condition vary
Tourism/casino proximity may affect lifestyle fit
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Strong first-look area for users who value commute simplicity and base proximity, as long as flood and property risk are controlled.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: D'Iberville
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Practical family-oriented option north of Biloxi with services, newer inventory in some pockets, and manageable commute.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 15-30 minutes depending on exact address, gate, traffic, and route
likely_gate_logic: Pass Road Gate depending on route
watchouts: Flood zones still need review
Newer subdivisions may include HOA costs
School assignment should be verified by exact address
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Strong practical option for families wanting services, commute balance, and less immediate casino/tourism exposure.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Gulfport Selected Pockets
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Broad housing market west of Biloxi with more inventory and services, but neighborhood and commute variation require screening.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 20-40 minutes depending on exact address, route, gate, and traffic
likely_gate_logic: White Avenue Gate depending on route
watchouts: Neighborhood quality varies significantly
Flood and insurance profile must be checked
Older homes may require roof/HVAC/plumbing review
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Good value and inventory option when exact neighborhood, condition, and commute fit are strong.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Long Beach
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Coastal west-side option with family appeal and reasonable Keesler commute, but insurance and flood profile matter.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 25-45 minutes depending on exact address, route, traffic, and gate
likely_gate_logic: White Avenue Gate depending on route
watchouts: Coastal flood and insurance risk must be reviewed
Commute should be tested during actual schedule windows
Property elevation and roof age matter
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Good coastal-family option when insurance and all-in payment remain disciplined.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Ocean Springs
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Higher-demand lifestyle corridor east of Biloxi with strong appeal, but pricing, commute, and insurance should be tested.
best_for: VA buyers with strong residual income
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 25-45+ minutes depending on exact address, bridge/traffic, gate, and route
likely_gate_logic: Pass Road Gate depending on route
watchouts: Prices may be higher than Biloxi/Gulfport options
Flood and insurance still matter
Bridge/route timing should be tested
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Strong lifestyle option only when payment, commute, and insurance math support it.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Woolmarket
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Inland Biloxi-area option with more space, suburban feel, and potentially less immediate coastal exposure.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 20-35 minutes depending on exact address, route, and gate
likely_gate_logic: Pass Road Gate depending on route
watchouts: HOA or newer subdivision costs may apply
Flood maps and drainage still require review
School assignment should be verified
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Good family and value option for users who want more space without moving too far from Keesler.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Vancleave / St. Martin
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: More rural/suburban east-side options with space and quieter lifestyle, but commute and services should be tested.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 30-50+ minutes depending on exact address, route, gate, and traffic
likely_gate_logic: Pass Road Gate depending on route
watchouts: Longer commute can affect daily life
Rural utilities, internet, flood/drainage, and services should be verified
Resale may be more specialized than central Biloxi
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Best for families intentionally seeking space and quiet, with commute and utilities accepted upfront.

commute_intelligence.overall_commute_posture: Generally manageable near Biloxi and D'Iberville, moderate from Gulfport, Long Beach, and Ocean Springs, and more commute-sensitive from rural or extended corridors.
primary_commute_risks: Flooded routes during storms or heavy rain
Choosing by beach/lifestyle instead of gate route
Longer drives from Ocean Springs, Vancleave, or far Gulfport
planning_guidance: Confirm the user’s actual duty location and primary gate before choosing a neighborhood.
Test the commute during the user’s expected report-time window.
Compare Biloxi, D'Iberville, Gulfport, Long Beach, Ocean Springs, Woolmarket, and St. Martin by route reliability, flood exposure, and school fit.
Check flood zones, insurance quotes, elevation, and roof age before treating a property as affordable.
Use commute plus all-in housing cost as the core PCSUnited decision screen.
commute_intelligence.pcsu_bluf: Keesler commute decisions are usually manageable, but flood-prone roads, gate route, casino/tourism traffic, and bridge/corridor timing can quietly change the best housing choice.

bah_market_reality.buying_power_summary: BAH can support buying in many Biloxi/Gulfport-area corridors because prices are lower than many coastal military markets, but insurance and flood exposure can change the answer quickly.
bah_market_reality.renting_summary: Renting can be smart for short training assignments, short timelines, or users uncertain about Gulf Coast flood/insurance exposure.
bah_market_reality.pcsu_strategy_note: Keesler should be modeled as a strong affordability market with coastal risk overlays. PCSUnited should test rank, BAH, debt, savings, credit score, family size, duty status, commute tolerance, flood exposure, insurance estimates, roof condition, and assignment length before recommending buy versus rent.

strengths: Affordable coastal housing compared with many military markets
Short commute options in Biloxi and D'Iberville
Strong base training and medical ecosystem
Access to beaches, seafood, Gulf Coast events, and regional amenities
Practical rental and ownership options when flood/insurance risk is controlled
watchouts: Insurance and flood risk must be verified
Storm readiness and evacuation planning matter
School assignments must be verified by exact address
Tourism/casino corridors may not fit every family
Roof age, HVAC, drainage, and elevation matter
family_readiness.pcsu_family_bluf: Keesler can be a strong family PCS location when the household chooses the right Gulf Coast corridor, verifies insurance and flood risk, and avoids weak-condition properties that look cheap upfront.

arrival_checklist: Confirm whether the user is in a short training pipeline, permanent party, or longer assignment before recommending buy versus rent.
Verify current gate hours and visitor access procedures through official Keesler AFB sources.
Contact the Keesler Military Housing Office early for current housing conditions and relocation support.
Compare Biloxi, D'Iberville, Gulfport, Long Beach, Ocean Springs, Woolmarket, St. Martin, and Vancleave using commute, flood risk, schools, safety, insurance, and all-in monthly cost.
Check flood zone, homeowners insurance, flood insurance, roof age, storm history, drainage, elevation, HVAC, and utility estimates before finalizing a purchase budget.
Verify school zones by exact property address.
Use PCSUnited calculators to compare rent, buy, residual income, insurance pressure, commute risk, and assignment-length exposure before committing.
pcs_watchouts: Do not buy only because the purchase price looks low.
Do not ignore insurance quotes, flood zone, roof age, elevation, or storm history.
Do not assume every Biloxi/Gulfport neighborhood has the same family or resale profile.
Do not rely only on mileage; gate route, flooding, and event/tourism traffic matter.
Short training timelines may favor renting or on-base housing.
A cheap home with a bad roof, flood exposure, or insurance issue can become more expensive than a higher-priced clean home.

realtor_intelligence.best_client_positioning: Lead with assignment timeline, BAH fit, flood/insurance clarity, roof condition, VA readiness, school-zone confidence, and commute strategy. Keesler military clients need coastal risk awareness with practical affordability math.
strong_listing_angles: Updated roof, HVAC, windows, drainage, and storm-readiness
Access to Keesler medical/training mission and Gulf Coast services
buyer_consultation_angles: Rent versus buy based on training/permanent-party timeline
All-in payment including taxes, insurance, flood insurance, utilities, and maintenance
Biloxi versus D'Iberville versus Gulfport versus Ocean Springs tradeoffs
Roof age, flood zone, elevation, and insurance quote review
Future resale appeal to military and Gulf Coast buyers
investor_consultation_angles: Military student and permanent-party renter demand
realtor_intelligence.pcsu_realtor_bluf: A strong Keesler realtor should sound like a coastal-risk advisor, training-timeline strategist, VA-condition filter, and insurance-aware financial guide — not just a Gulf Coast salesperson.

notes: Market and housing values are PCSUnited planning estimates and should be refreshed periodically.
Gate, visitor, service, medical, and housing office details should be verified against official installation sources before publication or use in routing.
Placeholder map image is used until a dedicated Keesler AFB base orientation map is created.
Keesler-area pricing and commute experience vary materially by Biloxi, D'Iberville, Gulfport, Long Beach, Ocean Springs, Woolmarket, and St. Martin.

source_notes: Use official Keesler AFB sources for gate hours, visitor control, newcomer guidance, and installation access.
Use official Air Force Housing, Military OneSource, Keesler Military Housing Office, and Keesler Family Housing sources for on-base housing and privatized housing details.
Use DeCA, AAFES, and TRICARE sources for commissary, exchange, and medical details.
Use Zillow-style indexes, Realtor.com-style market data, local MLS, rental aggregators, HUD, and Census-style demographic sources for housing and market refreshes.
base_profile.disclaimer: PCSUnited base profiles are planning tools only. They are not official DoD, Air Force, installation, legal, financial, real estate, lending, insurance, flood-risk, school-zone, or navigation guidance. Users should verify gate access, housing rules, school zones, medical services, visitor procedures, market prices, flood zones, insurance estimates, commute conditions, utility costs, roof age, elevation, storm history, and property condition through official and professional sources before making decisions.
